description_long: This object consists of two realistic carrots, each displaying a vibrant orange root with subtle surface striations and a slightly tapered, cylindrical form. One carrot has a small cluster of green leafy stems attached at the top, while the other is shown with a cleanly cut top, exposing the inner core. The root tips are mostly pointed, and the surface texture is matte with a natural, slightly rough appearance. The carrots are free of visible dirt and show no signs of branching or splitting. The coloration transitions smoothly from deep orange along the body to a lighter, yellow-orange at the cut ends and green at the leafy tops.
description: A pair of orange carrots, one with green leafy stems and one with a cut top, featuring a matte, slightly rough surface and pointed tips.
description_view_0: End-on view showing the cut top of one carrot and the leafy green stems of the other.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of both carrots, highlighting their tapered, cylindrical shape and attached leafy stems.
description_view_2: Opposite end-on view showing the pointed root tips and the leafy stems from above.
description_view_3: Side view from the opposite direction, again showing the full length and the leafy stems, with the cut top visible.
